title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Research, and Academic Profile
text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen MSc (Econ), PhD Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with particular expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work continues to shape debates on the resilience of green investments and the role of financial markets in sustainable growth. Role: Head of Economics Research Interests: Convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies, ESG finance and sustainable investing, Financial market complexity and behavioural dynamics, Volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness Department: Economics academics Academic profile Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is the founding Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He joined UoSD in June 2025. He received his PhD from West Bengal University of Technology (now MAKAUT) in 2012. He has previously received a BSc (H) in Economics and MSc in Economics from the University of Calcutta. Chitrakalpa’s primary research interest includes sustainable finance, green investments, market volatility, and the role of financial markets in fostering long-term resilience, alongside recent work in growth economics. His research is regularly published in leading international peer-reviewed journals. Chitrakalpa has over 14 years of teaching and research experience. Prior to joining University of Southampton Delhi, he has held senior academic and leadership positions at OP Jindal Global University and other leading Indian institutions. At Jindal Global Business School, he served as the senior associate dean of internationalization and mobility, where he was instrumental in advancing international partnerships, building student exchange pathways, and strengthening global research collaborations.
summary: Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen, Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi, holds a PhD and specializes in sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with extensive teaching and leadership experience.

title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen's Recent Academic Publications (2019-2025)
text:
Publications (Part 1)
Towards a Sustainable Future Bamel, N., Khatri, K. & Sen, C. (2025). “Towards a Sustainable Future: A Comprehensive Review and Future Directions in Sustainability Reporting Research”. Journal of Economic Surveys. Forthcoming.
Does Distance Matter in Convergence Among Indian States? Roy, S. & Sen, C. (2025). Does Distance Matter in Convergence Among Indian States? Asian Development Review.
Beyond the glitter: An empirical assessment of the true risk and hedging role of precious metals Sen, C., & Chakrabarti, G. (2024). “Beyond the glitter: An empirical assessment of the true risk and hedging role of precious metals”. Resources Policy, 96, 105238.
Exploring the risk dynamics of US green energy stocks: A green time-varying beta approach Sen, C., & Chakrabarti, G. (2024). “Exploring the risk dynamics of US green energy stocks: A green time-varying beta approach”. Energy Economics, 139, 107951.
Performance, Pandemic and Probable Peril Sen. C. and Chakrabarti, G., (2023), “Performance, Pandemic and Probable Peril: Disentangling Risk, Volatility and Bubbles in FAANG Portfolio”, New York Economic Review, Vol. 53, Fall 2023, pp.115-126
Role of debt in emerging economies: a re-examination of the debt-growth nexus Roy, S., and Sen. C., (2022). “Role of debt in emerging economies: A re-examination of the debt-growth nexus”. Macroeconomics and Finance in Emerging Market Economies, pp. 1–22.
Dynamic market risk of green stocks across regions: Where does the devil lie? Chakrabarti, G. and C. Sen, (2021), “Dynamic market risk of green stocks across regions: Where does the devil lie?”, Journal of Cleaner Production, Vol. 303, 127028.
An Empirical Inquiry into Per Capita Convergence of Indian States Roy, S., Sen, C. and R. Sanyal (2019) “An empirical inquiry into per capita convergence of Indian states”, Global Journal of Emerging Market Economies, Vol. 11, No. 3, pp. 232-247.
summary: This section lists Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen's recent publications from 2019 to 2025, covering sustainable finance, green energy stocks, emerging market economics, and convergence dynamics.
entities: Macroeconomics and Finance in Emerging Market Economies
Global Journal of Emerging Market Economies

title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen's Earlier Academic Publications (2009-2018)
text:
Publications (Part 2)
Pricing of Green Stocks in India G. Chakrabarti and C. Sen, (2018) “Pricing of Green Stocks in India”, Empirical Economics Letters, Vol. 7, No. 4.
Study of complexity and allied properties of financial markets Mukherjee I., Sen, C., Sarkar, A. and A. Giri (2016) “Study of complexity and allied properties of financial markets – theoretical approaches, empirical observations and use of the tools of non-linear dynamics” Chaos and Complexity Letters, International Journal of Dynamical Systems Research, Vol. 10, Issue 1, pp. 37-63.
The efficacy of intervention in a chaotic foreign exchange market: an empirical study in INR-USD exchange rate series Chakrabarti G. and C. Sen, (2014), “The Efficacy of Intervention in a Chaotic Foreign Exchange Market: An Empirical Study in INR-USD Exchange Rate Series”, Decision, Indian Institute of Management Calcutta, 41(4), pp. 399-410.
FDI in the Service Sector Sen, (2011), “FDI in the Service Sector – Propagator of Growth for India?” Theoretical and Applied Economics, Volume XVIII, No. 6(559), pp. 141-156.
Long Memory in Stock Returns: Insights from the Indian Market Mukherjee I., Sen C. and A. Sarkar, (2011), “Long Memory in Stock Returns: Insights from the Indian Market”, International Journal of Applied Economics and Finance, Volume 5, Number 1, pp. 62-74.
Study of Stylized Facts in Indian Financial Markets Mukherjee I. Sen C. and A. Sarkar, (2011), “Study of Stylized Facts in Indian Financial Markets”, International Journal of Applied Economics and Finance, Volume 5, Number 1, pp. 127-137.
Volatility Regimes and Calendar Anomaly in Foreign Exchange Market Chakrabarti G and C. Sen, (2011), “Volatility Regimes and Calendar Anomaly in Foreign Exchange Market”, International Journal of Applied Economics and Finance, Volume 5, Number 2, pp. 97-113.
Evidence of chaos: A Tale of Two Exchange Rates Sen, C., Chakrabarti, G. and A. Sarkar, (2011), “Evidence of chaos: A Tale of Two Exchange Rates”, The Empirical Economics Letters, Volume 10, No. 8, pp. 777-784.
Indian stock market volatility in recent years: Transmission from global market, regional market and traditional domestic sectors Sarkar A., Chakrabarti G. and C. Sen, (2009), “Indian Stock Market Volatility in Recent Years: Transmission from Global Market, Regional Market and Traditional Domestic Sectors”, Journal of Asset Management 10, pp. 63-71.
Volatility in Indian Stock Market in Recent Years Chakrabarti G., Sen C. and A. Sarkar, (2012), “Volatility in Indian Stock Market in Recent Years: Transmission from Domestic Sectors”, Finance India, XXVI (3).
summary: This section details Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen's publications from 2009 to 2018, encompassing green stock pricing, financial market complexity, foreign exchange dynamics, and Indian stock market volatility.
entities: International Journal of Dynamical Systems Research
International Journal of Applied Economics and Finance

title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen's Ongoing Research Project on ESG Disclosure
text:
Research projects
Examining the Impact of Firm Attributes on ESG Disclosure Scores “Examining the Impact of Firm Attributes on ESG Disclosure Scores” with Komal Khatri (ongoing).
summary: Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen is currently conducting an ongoing research project with Komal Khatri, focusing on the impact of firm attributes on ESG disclosure scores.

title: Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen: Head of Economics, Profile, and Research
text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen MSc (Econ), PhD is Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi. He holds a PhD in Economics from the West Bengal University of Technology, following an MSc and BSc (H) in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His research lies at the intersection of sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with expertise in volatility modelling, regime switching, and convergence in emerging economies. Chitrakalpa has published widely in leading international journals such as Journal of Economic Surveys, Energy Economics, Resources Policy and the Journal of Cleaner Production, and his work shapes debates on green investments and financial markets in sustainable growth. Role: Head of Economics. Research Interests include convergence and growth dynamics in emerging economies, ESG finance, sustainable investing, financial market complexity, behavioural dynamics, volatility modelling, regime-switching, and higher-order moment connectedness. Department: Economics academics. Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is the founding Head of Economics at the University of Southampton Delhi, joining in June 2025. He received his PhD from West Bengal University of Technology (now MAKAUT) in 2012, and BSc (H) and MSc in Economics from the University of Calcutta. His primary research interest spans sustainable finance, green investments, market volatility, fostering long-term resilience, and growth economics. He has over 14 years of teaching and research experience, previously holding senior academic and leadership positions at OP Jindal Global University, where he was instrumental in advancing international partnerships and research collaborations. Current research projects include “Examining the Impact of Firm Attributes on ESG Disclosure Scores” with Komal Khatri.
summary: Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen is the Head of Economics at UoSD, specializing in sustainable finance, ESG investing, and financial market dynamics, with extensive academic and leadership experience.

title: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen: Name Aliases and Alternate Spellings
text: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen is a faculty member at University of Southampton Delhi. This person may also be referred to by the following names or spellings: Dr Chitrakalpa Sen, Dr. Chitrakalpa Sen, Chitrakalpa Sen, C. Sen, Chitrakalpa Senn, Chitrakalpa Shen, Dr Chitrakalpa Senn, Chitra Kalpa Sen, Dr. Chitrakalpa Shen. All of these names refer to the same individual.
summary: Alias resolution chunk for Dr Chitrakalpa Sen. Lists all known alternate spellings and name variants to prevent retrieval errors caused by misspellings.
